points: Perimeter is the total distance covered along the outer boundary of a closed plane figure.
For a rectangle, the formula is $2 \times (\text{length} + \text{breadth})$. For a square, it is $4 \times \text{length of a side}$.
The perimeter of any regular polygon is the number of sides multiplied by the length of one side (e.g., $3 \times \text{side}$ for an equilateral triangle).
Area is the amount of surface region enclosed within a closed figure, generally measured in square units.
The area of a rectangle is $\text{length} \times \text{breadth}$, and the area of a square is $\text{side} \times \text{side}$.
Area of irregular shapes can be estimated using grid paper by counting full squares, ignoring small fragments, and rounding larger ones.
A rectangle can be divided by a diagonal into two equal triangles, each having half the area of the rectangle.
Shapes with the same area can have different perimeters (e.g., 9 unit squares can form perimeters ranging from 12 to 20 units).
Two figures can have the same perimeter but enclose different amounts of area depending on their specific dimensions.
Perimeter is used in real-life tasks like finding the length of lace for a cloth or the distance covered when running around a park.
Area concepts are applied in tiling floors, carpeting rooms, or determining the number of trees that can be planted in a grove.
Complex problems often involve finding the 'remaining area' by subtracting one shape's area from another (e.g., a carpet on a floor).
